steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to compare Rosen's theory with Lowell's. A valid answer must name both theorists' positions and directly contrast them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — In repositioning the screenwriter (not the director) as the primary author of a film, schreiber theory presents an alternative to the dominant auteur theory.: Directly contrasts Rosen's schreiber theory (screenwriter as primary author) with Lowell's auteur theory (director as primary author). Both theories are compared. ✓
  • Choice B — Film critics Martin Lowell and Daniel Rosen hold the view that film authorship should be shared among those involved in the filmmaking process.: Misrepresents both theorists — Lowell and Rosen each argue for a single primary author, not shared authorship. That's Hart's position. ✗
  • Choice C — In a 1963 rebuttal to Lowell's theory that a director is the primary author of a film, Hart proposed that screenwriters should instead be given sole authorship.: Compares Lowell and Hart, not Lowell and Rosen. Also misrepresents Hart, who argued for shared authorship, not screenwriter sole authorship. ✗
  • Choice D — Rosen's theory of film authorship contrasts with that of Hart, who famously argued against Lowell's auteur theory in 1963.: Compares Rosen with Hart, not with Lowell. The required comparison is between Rosen and Lowell. ✗
That matches Choice A — In repositioning the screenwriter (not the director) as the primary author of a film, schreiber theory presents an alternative to the dominant auteur theory..
